sources.notes: Interactive query task about recovering bitwise information.
Official tutorial recovers bits group by group with adaptive queries.
Maspy describes determining the high bit groups using potentialized union-find and recursing to lower bits inside groups.

annotation.summary: Recover enough hidden bitwise information through adaptive comparison queries.
annotation.constraints: The query budget is small, so each query must split candidates by high bits efficiently.
annotation.core_idea: First determine the most significant bit groups. Query endpoints and compare other elements to a far endpoint; equality/difference of the high bit induces parity constraints, which can be maintained as a potentialized union-find. After splitting by bit 29, recurse inside each group for bit 28 and below. Random/probabilistic estimates justify that the expected group sizes and query costs fit the limit.
annotation.complexity: Interactive query budget around O(n log V) with small constants.
tricks: recover bits from high to low

solution_variants.name: bitwise-group-interactive
solution_variants.summary: Split candidates by high-bit equality constraints using interactive queries and recurse on lower bits in each group.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n log V) queries with small constants

tags.evidence: The solution is an adaptive query strategy.
The hidden information is recovered bit by bit from the most significant bit downward.
Potentialized union-find maintains equality/parity relations inferred from queries.
